purpose: W1 J-DATABASE projector and snapshot verification fixtures

event_envelope.sequence_semantics: Positive integer, unique and strictly increasing per run.
event_envelope.type_semantics: Exactly three phase-qualified dot segments matching actor.role and normalized actor.phase.
event_envelope.payload_semantics: Event-specific JSON object persisted without lossy transformation.

w0_contract.sequence_allocator: packages/contracts EventSequenceAllocator owns durable per-run allocation; the emitter serializes allocation plus append on the event log guard.
w0_contract.timestamps: RFC 3339 occurred_at strings are stored as PostgreSQL timestamptz without changing instants.
w0_contract.identities: actor.agent_id is the persistent logical identity. Phase changes never create another agents row.
w0_contract.notifications: One post-commit run_events notification is emitted only for a newly inserted event. Replay conflicts produce no additional notification.
w0_contract.cursor: The durable cursor records byte offset, last sequence, and last event id in the same transaction as projections.

scenarios.name: canonical
covers: one reviewer identity across initial_review, followup, and discussion

scenarios.name: out-of-order
covers: transactional rejection without projection or notification

scenarios.name: crash-restart
covers: restart replay of the committed event
no loss, duplication, or duplicate notification
